This run (2026-07-20): seq 422-424, three authority posts across three pillars, all site/blog/. Pipeline hygiene (Sales Pipeline Management, guide, global; Validity's State of CRM Data Management in 2025 report cited, 602 CRM users/admins across US/UK/Australia, July 2025 (76% of organisations say less than half their CRM data is accurate and complete, average loss of 16 sales opportunities a quarter), Salesforce's State of Sales report 2024 edition cited (only 35% of sales professionals fully trust their pipeline data); five-step weekly hygiene routine plus a four-metric tracking section (update recency, next-step coverage, stage ageing, forecast variance), worked example of an eight-person SaaS team's 22 stale deals; links to sales-pipeline-management.html (pillar) and sales-dashboards.html (money, live), both live and used, matching the sibling post sales-pipeline-review.html's pattern; lateral to sales-pipeline-review.html and healthy-sales-pipeline.html). B2B buyer personas (Go-to-Market Strategy, guide, global; Gartner's B2B buying journey research cited (typical complex buying group of six to ten decision makers, each buyer spends only around 17% of the purchase journey meeting suppliers); five-part persona framework (trigger, evaluation criteria, internal objection, information source, reason deals die) plus a deal-size scaling section; worked example of a twelve-person software company surfacing a hidden vetoing persona; links to go-to-market-strategy.html only, positioning-and-messaging-service money page not yet live so single up-link, matching precedent (what-is-positioning.html); lateral to what-is-positioning.html and sales-playbook.html). The conversion rate formula, with examples (Conversion & Funnel Optimisation, definition, global; IRP Commerce's ecommerce market data cited (UK all-industry average site-wide conversion rate around 1.6-1.9% into early 2026) and Littledata's 2,800-Shopify-store benchmark cited (median around 2.5-2.8%, top 20% above 3.2%); formula applied across three business models (ecommerce, SaaS trial, B2B lead-gen) plus a stage-by-stage worked example (2% headline rate resolving into a 12.5% add-to-basket rate and an 84% checkout abandonment problem); links to conversion-rate-optimisation.html and conversion-rate-optimisation-services.html, both live, exactly as specified in the plan record; lateral to how-to-calculate-conversion-rate.html and what-is-a-good-conversion-rate.html, deliberately differentiated in angle (definition/snippet-focused vs that post's tracking-and-segmentation method) to avoid overlap given the closely related topics).
